description: Search and filter the public model catalog
steps: Open Hub — you land on the All Models view by default
Use the search bar to filter by model name
Apply filters: Tags (vision, NLP, audio), Worlds (deployment context), Hardware (optimized hardware), Utilities (runtime compatibility)
Stack multiple filters to narrow results
Bookmark the URL — it updates as you filter so the filtered view is shareable
explanation: Hub catalog shows every publicly available model across all organizations by default. Switch to your org catalog via the org switcher to see private models.

validation.rule: Model cards matching your search/filters appear on screen; active filter chips shown above results

common_errors: Private models not visible — you must switch to your org via the org switcher in the toolbar
Filters not clearing — click 'Clear all' above the filter chips

description: Understand a model's detail page (5 tabs)

tabs.Overview: What the model does, its capabilities, and what hardware it targets
tabs.Files: All downloadable files: weights, configs, and assets
tabs.Installation: Step-by-step setup instructions for getting the model running locally
tabs.Usage: Code examples and how to run the model for inference
tabs.Diagnostics: Technical health indicators, runtime metadata, compatibility notes. Also used to monitor live fine-tuning jobs.

steps: Click any model card in the catalog
Start at the Overview tab to understand the model
Go to Installation and Usage when ready to work with it
Check Diagnostics for training job status if the model is currently being fine-tuned
expected_outcome: You understand what the model does and how to use it

validation.rule: User can answer: what does this model do? what hardware does it need? how do I install it?

description: Download a model file from the Files tab
steps: Click the download button next to the file you need
Hub generates a secure, time-limited link and starts the download automatically
explanation: Download links are time-limited. If a download doesn't start, click the button again to generate a fresh link.
expected_outcome: File download begins immediately in your browser

validation.rule: Downloaded file exists on disk (check your browser's downloads folder)

common_errors: Download didn't start — link may have expired; click the download button again to regenerate
Private model not appearing — switch org via the org switcher in toolbar

description: Switch to your org's private model catalog
steps: Locate the Organization selector in the Hub toolbar
Click it and select your team organization from the dropdown
The catalog now shows only that org's models (including private ones)
explanation: By default you're viewing the public catalog. Private models only appear when scoped to the correct org. You must be a member of the org.

validation.rule: Org name shown in the toolbar selector; model cards shown are scoped to that org

common_errors: Org not in dropdown — you may not be a member; ask an Admin to invite you
Still seeing all models — confirm the selector changed and the page refreshed
